   107→func (m model) handleKeyPress(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
   108→	switch {
   109→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Quit):
   110→		return m, tea.Quit
   111→
   112→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Help):
   113→		m.showHelp = !m.showHelp
   114→		return m, nil
   115→
   116→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Refresh):
   117→		return m, m.loadCheckpoints()
   118→
   119→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Filter):
   120→		if m.screen == screenOverview {
   121→			m.filtering = true
   122→			return m, nil
   123→		}
   124→	}
   125→
   126→	switch m.screen {
   127→	case screenOverview:
   128→		return m.handleOverviewKeys(msg)
   129→	case screenDetail:
   130→		return m.handleDetailKeys(msg)
   131→	}
   132→
   133→	return m, nil
   134→}
   135→
   136→func (m model) handleOverviewKeys(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
   137→	filtered := filterSummaries(m.summaries, m.filter)
   138→
   139→	switch {
   140→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Down):
   141→		if m.cursor < len(filtered)-1 {
   142→			m.cursor++
   143→		}
   144→
   145→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Up):
   146→		if m.cursor > 0 {
   147→			m.cursor--
   148→		}
   149→
   150→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Enter):
   151→		if len(filtered) > 0 && m.cursor < len(filtered) {
   152→			selected := filtered[m.cursor]
   153→			m.screen = screenDetail
   154→			m.detail = nil
   155→			m.transcript = nil
   156→			m.detailTab = tabOverview
   157→			m.scrollOffset = 0
   158→			return m, tea.Batch(
   159→				m.loadDetail(selected.ID),
   160→				m.loadTranscript(selected.ID),
   161→			)
   162→		}
   163→	}
   164→
   165→	return m, nil
   166→}
   167→
   168→func (m model) handleDetailKeys(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
   169→	switch {
   170→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Back):
   171→		m.screen = screenOverview
   172→		m.detail = nil
   173→		m.transcript = nil
   174→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   175→		return m, nil
   176→
   177→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Tab):
   178→		m.detailTab = (m.detailTab + 1) % 4
   179→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   180→		return m, nil
   181→
   182→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.ShiftTab):
   183→		m.detailTab = (m.detailTab + 3) % 4
   184→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   185→		return m, nil
   186→
   187→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Num1):
   188→		m.detailTab = tabOverview
   189→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   190→		return m, nil
   191→
   192→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Num2):
   193→		m.detailTab = tabFiles
   194→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   195→		return m, nil
   196→
   197→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Num3):
   198→		m.detailTab = tabTranscript
   199→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   200→		return m, nil
   201→
   202→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Num4):
   203→		m.detailTab = tabPlan
   204→		m.scrollOffset = 0
   205→		return m, nil
   206→
   207→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Down):
   208→		if m.scrollOffset < m.maxScroll {
   209→			m.scrollOffset++
   210→		}
   211→		return m, nil
   212→
   213→	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Up):
   214→		if m.scrollOffset > 0 {
   215→			m.scrollOffset--
   216→		}
   217→		return m, nil
   218→	}
   219→
   220→	return m, nil
   221→}
   222→
   223→func (m model) handleFilterInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
   224→	switch msg.Type {
   225→	case tea.KeyEnter, tea.KeyEscape:
   226→		m.filtering = false
   227→		if msg.Type == tea.KeyEscape {
   228→			m.filter = ""
   229→		}
   230→		m.cursor = 0
   231→		return m, nil
   232→	case tea.KeyBackspace:
   233→		if m.filter != "" {
   234→			m.filter = m.filter[:len(m.filter)-1]
   235→		}
   236→		m.cursor = 0
   237→		return m, nil
   238→	default:
   239→		if len(msg.Runes) > 0 {
   240→			m.filter += string(msg.Runes)
   241→			m.cursor = 0
   242→		}
   243→		return m, nil
   244→	}
   245→}

   247→func (m model) renderOverviewScreen() string {
   248→	panelWidth := m.width - 2
   249→
   250→	overview := renderOverview(m.stats, panelWidth-2)
   251→	overviewPanel := panelStyle.Width(panelWidth).Render(overview)
   252→	overviewHeight := lipgloss.Height(overviewPanel)
   253→
   254→	footerContent := m.renderFooterContent()
   255→	footerPanel := panelStyle.Width(panelWidth).Render(footerContent)
   256→	footerHeight := lipgloss.Height(footerPanel)
   257→
   258→	listPanelOuter := m.height - overviewHeight - footerHeight
   259→	if listPanelOuter < 5 {
   260→		listPanelOuter = 5
   261→	}
   262→	listInnerHeight := listPanelOuter - 2
   263→	if listInnerHeight < 3 {
   264→		listInnerHeight = 3
   265→	}
   266→
   267→	contentWidth := panelWidth - 2
   268→	list := renderCheckpointList(m.summaries, m.cursor, listInnerHeight, m.filter, contentWidth)
   269→	listPanel := panelStyle.Width(panelWidth).Height(listPanelOuter - 2).Render(list)
   270→
   271→	return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, overviewPanel, listPanel, footerPanel)
   272→}
   273→
   274→func (m model) renderDetailScreen() string {
   275→	if m.detail == nil {
   276→		return dimStyle.Render("Loading checkpoint...")
   277→	}
   278→
   279→	panelWidth := m.width - 2
   280→
   281→	header := renderDetailHeader(m.detail, panelWidth-2)
   282→	headerPanel := panelStyle.Width(panelWidth).Render(header)
   283→	headerHeight := lipgloss.Height(headerPanel)
   284→
   285→	footerContent := dimStyle.Render("esc back  j/k scroll  tab switch  1-4 tabs  ? help  q quit")
   286→	footerPanel := panelStyle.Width(panelWidth).Render(footerContent)
   287→	footerHeight := lipgloss.Height(footerPanel)
   288→
   289→	contentOuter := m.height - headerHeight - footerHeight
   290→	if contentOuter < 5 {
   291→		contentOuter = 5
   292→	}
   293→	contentInner := contentOuter - 2
   294→	if contentInner < 3 {
   295→		contentInner = 3
   296→	}
   297→
   298→	tabBar := renderTabBar(m.detailTab)
   299→	tabBarHeight := lipgloss.Height(tabBar) + 1
   300→
   301→	tabContentHeight := contentInner - tabBarHeight
   302→	innerWidth := panelWidth - 2
   303→
   304→	var tabContent string
   305→	switch m.detailTab {
   306→	case tabOverview:
   307→		tabContent = renderTabOverview(m.detail, innerWidth, tabContentHeight)
   308→	case tabFiles:
   309→		tabContent = renderTabFiles(m.detail.Diff, innerWidth, tabContentHeight)
   310→	case tabTranscript:
   311→		tabContent = renderTabTranscript(m.transcript, innerWidth, tabContentHeight, m.scrollOffset)
   312→	case tabPlan:
   313→		tabContent = renderTabPlan(m.detail.Plan, innerWidth, tabContentHeight)
   314→	}
   315→
   316→	if m.detailTab != tabTranscript {
   317→		lines := strings.Split(tabContent, "\n")
   318→		maxScroll := len(lines) - tabContentHeight
   319→		if maxScroll < 0 {
   320→			maxScroll = 0
   321→		}
   322→		scrollOffset := m.scrollOffset
   323→		if scrollOffset > maxScroll {
   324→			scrollOffset = maxScroll
   325→		}
   326→		end := scrollOffset + tabContentHeight
   327→		if end > len(lines) {
   328→			end = len(lines)
   329→		}
   330→		if scrollOffset < len(lines) {
   331→			tabContent = strings.Join(lines[scrollOffset:end], "\n")
   332→		}
   333→	}
   334→
   335→	combined := lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, tabBar, "", tabContent)
   336→	contentPanel := panelStyle.Width(panelWidth).Height(contentOuter - 2).Render(combined)
   337→
   338→	return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, headerPanel, contentPanel, footerPanel)
   339→}
   340→
   341→func (m model) renderFooterContent() string {
   342→	if m.filtering {
   343→		return fmt.Sprintf("Filter: %s█", m.filter)
   344→	}
   345→
   346→	leftSide := fmt.Sprintf("%d checkpoints", m.stats.TotalCheckpoints)
   347→	if m.stats.TotalTokens > 0 {
   348→		leftSide += "  ·  " + formatTokensShort(m.stats.TotalTokens)
   349→	}
   350→
   351→	rightSide := "j/k navigate  enter select  / filter  ? help  q quit"
   352→
   353→	padding := m.width - 6 - lipgloss.Width(leftSide) - lipgloss.Width(rightSide)
   354→	if padding < 2 {
   355→		padding = 2
   356→	}
   357→
   358→	return leftSide + strings.Repeat(" ", padding) + dimStyle.Render(rightSide)
   359→}
   360→
   361→func (m model) loadCheckpoints() tea.Cmd {
   362→	return func() tea.Msg {
   363→		summaries, err := m.loader.ListCheckpoints()
   364→		if err != nil {
   365→			return errMsg{err}
   366→		}
   367→		return checkpointsLoadedMsg{summaries}
   368→	}
   369→}
   370→
   371→func (m model) loadDetail(id string) tea.Cmd {
   372→	return func() tea.Msg {
   373→		cp, err := m.loader.LoadCheckpoint(id)
   374→		if err != nil {
   375→			return errMsg{err}
   376→		}
   377→		return checkpointDetailLoadedMsg{cp}
   378→	}
   379→}
   380→
   381→func (m model) loadTranscript(id string) tea.Cmd {
   382→	return func() tea.Msg {
   383→		entries, err := m.loader.LoadTranscript(id)
   384→		if err != nil {
   385→			return errMsg{err}
   386→		}
   387→		return transcriptLoadedMsg{entries}
   388→	}
   389→}

package checkpoint

import "time"

type TokenUsage struct {
	InputTokens          int `json:"input_tokens"`
	CacheCreationTokens  int `json:"cache_creation_tokens"`
	CacheReadTokens      int `json:"cache_read_tokens"`
	OutputTokens         int `json:"output_tokens"`
	APICallCount         int `json:"api_call_count"`
}

type SessionRef struct {
	Metadata    string `json:"metadata"`
	Transcript  string `json:"transcript"`
	Context     string `json:"context"`
	ContentHash string `json:"content_hash"`
	Prompt      string `json:"prompt"`
}

type Attribution struct {
	AgentLines      int     `json:"agent_lines"`
	HumanAdded      int     `json:"human_added"`
	HumanModified   int     `json:"human_modified"`
	TotalCommitted  int     `json:"total_committed"`
	AgentPercentage float64 `json:"agent_percentage"`
}

type Metadata struct {
	CheckpointID     string       `json:"checkpoint_id"`
	CLIVersion       string       `json:"cli_version"`
	Strategy         string       `json:"strategy"`
	Branch           string       `json:"branch"`
	CheckpointsCount int          `json:"checkpoints_count"`
	FilesTouched     []string     `json:"files_touched"`
	Sessions         []SessionRef `json:"sessions"`
	TokenUsage       TokenUsage   `json:"token_usage"`
}

type SessionMetadata struct {
	CheckpointID string      `json:"checkpoint_id"`
	SessionID    string      `json:"session_id"`
	Strategy     string      `json:"strategy"`
	CreatedAt    string      `json:"created_at"`
	Branch       string      `json:"branch"`
	FilesTouched []string    `json:"files_touched"`
	Agent        string      `json:"agent"`
	TurnID       string      `json:"turn_id"`
	TokenUsage   TokenUsage  `json:"token_usage"`
	Attribution  Attribution `json:"initial_attribution"`
}

type Checkpoint struct {
	Metadata
	Session SessionMetadata
	Prompt  string
	Context string
	Diff    string
	Plan    string
}

type CheckpointSummary struct {
	ID            string
	CreatedAt     time.Time
	Branch        string
	Agent         string
	AgentPercent  int
	CommitHash    string
	CommitMessage string
	Author        string
	Context       string
	Prompt        string
	TotalTokens   int
	Duration      string
	FileCount     int
	Additions     int
	Deletions     int
	SessionCount  int
}

type TranscriptEntry struct {
	Type      string
	Role      string
	Content   string
	ToolName  string
	Timestamp time.Time
	Tokens    int
}

input.prompt:
I'm investigating why the transcript display in this TUI app is missing content from the beginning. 

The transcript comes from JSONL files stored on a git orphan branch. I need to understand the structure of the beginning of these JSONL files.

1. First, look at `pkg/checkpoint/jsonl.go` to understand how entries are parsed (especially `extractContent` and `ParseTranscript`)
2. Then look at `pkg/ui/dashboard/transcript.go` to see how `groupTranscriptEntries` filters entries - specifically what gets dropped
3. Try to find a real JSONL transcript by:
   - Check if the orphan branch `entire` exists: run `git branch -a | grep entire`
   - If it exists, list the tree: `git ls-tree --name-only entire` to find a checkpoint
   - Then read the first ~20 lines of a `full.jsonl` file using `git show entire:<path>/0/full.jsonl | head -20`
   - Show me the raw JSON of those first entries so I can see their structure

4. Compare the raw JSONL entry structure with what `extractContent` handles and what `groupTranscriptEntries` filters. Identify exactly which entries at the beginning are being dropped and why.

This is research only - don't modify any files.

# Plan: Improve `subtle` Color for Readability

## Context
The `subtle` color variable (`lipgloss.Color("241")`) is too dark on dark terminal backgrounds. It's used for panel borders, stat labels, dim text, inactive tabs, hash styles, help descriptions, tool badges, scroll indicators, and more — all of which are hard to read.

## Change

### `pkg/ui/dashboard/styles.go` — line 11

Change `subtle` from `"241"` to `"245"`:

```go
subtle = lipgloss.Color("245")
```

`"241"` is ~#626262 (very dark gray). `"245"` is ~#8a8a8a (medium gray) — readable on dark backgrounds while still clearly secondary to primary content.

### Styles affected (all via the `subtle` variable, no other changes needed)
- `panelStyle` / `statCardStyle` border color
- `statLabelStyle`, `hashStyle`, `dimStyle`, `helpDescStyle`, `toolBadgeStyle`, `tabInactiveStyle`, `scrollIndicatorStyle` text color

## Files Modified
- `pkg/ui/dashboard/styles.go` — one line change

## Verification
- `make build`
- Visual: labels, borders, and dim text are noticeably more readable on dark backgrounds

Excellent! Now I have the data I need. Let me create a comprehensive analysis document. Let me compile all my findings:

## Summary of Findings

Based on my investigation of the JSONL transcript structure and the code, here's what I've discovered about missing content at the beginning of transcripts:

### 1. JSONL Entry Structure

The raw JSONL file from the git orphan branch (`entire/checkpoints/v1:0d/c3c28f0aef/0/full.jsonl`) shows these entry types in order:
1. `file-history-snapshot` - metadata snapshot
2. `progress` - hook progress events  
3. `user` - user messages (with `message.role == "user"` and `message.content`)
4. `assistant` - assistant responses
5. Various other types like `system`, tool results, etc.

### 2. The Core Problem

Looking at `groupTranscriptEntries()` in `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/ui/dashboard/transcript.go` (lines 49-116):

**Entries that get silently dropped:**

1. **Lines 68-71**: Entries with `role == "human"` or `role == "user"` are SKIPPED if `e.Content == ""`:
   ```go
   case e.Role == "human" || e.Role == "user":
       if e.Content == "" {
           continue
       }
   ```

2. **Lines 86-89**: Entries with `role == "assistant"` are SKIPPED if `e.Content == ""`:
   ```go
   case e.Role == "assistant":
       if e.Content == "" {
           continue
       }
   ```

3. **Lines 104-111**: All other entries (those without explicit role matching) are SKIPPED if `e.Content == ""`:
   ```go
   default:
       if e.Content != "" {
           flushTools()
           groups = append(groups, messageGroup{...})
       }
   ```

### 3. How Content Gets Extracted

In `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/checkpoint/jsonl.go`, `extractContent()` (lines 97-156) handles these fields in priority order:

1. **`contentBlocks`** array (lines 100-111)
2. **`message`** field parsed as `messageContent` (lines 113-126) 
3. **`message`** field parsed as raw string (lines 128-131)
4. **`content`** field parsed as `contentBlock` array (lines 134-146)
5. **`content`** field parsed as raw string (lines 149-152)
6. Returns empty string `""` if nothing matches (line 155)

### 4. Entry Types That Likely Return Empty Content

The JSONL structure shows entries like:
- `file-history-snapshot` - no content fields
- `progress` - no content fields (contains metadata like `data.type == "hook_progress"`)
- `system` entries - may not have content

When `ParseTranscript()` (line 78) calls `extractContent()` on these entries, they return `"", ""` (empty content and no tool name).

### 5. The Filtering Impact

In the real JSONL from the checkpoint:
- **Entry 1**: `file-history-snapshot` → `extractContent()` returns `""` → `groupTranscriptEntries()` skips it
- **Entry 2**: `progress` with `type: "hook_progress"` → `extractContent()` returns `""` → skipped  
- **Entry 3**: `user` with actual plan content → `extractContent()` processes `message.content` → included
- **Entry 4+**: Assistant and subsequent messages → processed normally

**Result**: The first 1-2 entries (metadata/progress) are silently dropped because they don't match any content extraction pattern and have empty content, so `groupTranscriptEntries()` never creates message groups for them.

### 6. Files Involved

- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/checkpoint/jsonl.go` - Parsing and content extraction
- `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/ui/dashboard/transcript.go` - Filtering and grouping

The issue is intentional filtering of entries with empty content, but it silently drops metadata entries that legitimately have no displayable content, making it appear the transcript is incomplete.

   ```go
   case e.Role == "human" || e.Role == "user":
       if e.Content == "" {

       }
   ```

2. **Lines 86-89**: Entries with `role == "assistant"` are SKIPPED if `e.Content == ""`:
   ```go

       if e.Content == "" {
           continue
       }
   ```

3. **Lines 104-111**: All other entries (those without explicit role matching) are SKIPPED if `e.Content == ""`:
   ```go

       if e.Content != "" {
           flushTools()
           groups = append(groups, messageGroup{...})
       }
   ```

### 3. How Content Gets Extracted

In `/Users/arvos/Tactic/workspaces/workspace-entire-local-cli/entire-local/pkg/checkpoint/jsonl.go`, `extractContent()` (lines 97-156) handles these fields in priority order:

1. **`contentBlocks`** array (lines 100-111)
2. **`message`** field parsed as `messageContent` (lines 113-126) 
3. **`message`** field parsed as raw string (lines 128-131)
4. **`content`** field parsed as `contentBlock` array (lines 134-146)
5. **`content`** field parsed as raw string (lines 149-152)
6. Returns empty string `""` if nothing matches (line 155)

The JSONL structure shows entries like:
- `file-history-snapshot` - no content fields
- `progress` - no content fields (contains metadata like `data.type == "hook_progress"`)
- `system` entries - may not have content

# Plan: Show Transcript in Overview + Fix Missing Beginning

## Context
The Overview tab shows redundant Prompt & Context sections. Instead, show the transcript directly in Overview. But first, the transcript is missing its beginning: the first user message has `message.content` as a **plain string**, and `extractContent` in `jsonl.go` doesn't handle that format — it only handles `message.content` as `[]contentBlock`.

## Root Cause
In `pkg/checkpoint/jsonl.go` `extractContent()` (lines 113-131):
```go
if entry.Message != nil {
    var mc messageContent        // mc.Content is []contentBlock
    if json.Unmarshal(entry.Message, &mc) == nil && len(mc.Content) > 0 {
        // works for assistant messages where content is [{type:"text",text:"..."}]
    }
    var s string
    if json.Unmarshal(entry.Message, &s) == nil {
        // only works if message itself is a raw string — never true, it's always an object
    }
}
```
The first user message looks like: `{"role":"user","content":"the prompt text"}` — content is a string, not an array. `json.Unmarshal` into `messageContent` fails because `[]contentBlock` can't hold a string. The string fallback also fails because `message` is an object, not a string. Result: `""` returned, entry dropped.

## Changes

### 1. `pkg/checkpoint/jsonl.go` — Fix `extractContent` to handle string content in message objects

Add a new struct and fallback after the `messageContent` attempt fails:

```go
// After the existing messageContent attempt (line 126), add:
var ms struct {
    Role    string `json:"role"`
    Content string `json:"content"`
}
if json.Unmarshal(entry.Message, &ms) == nil && ms.Content != "" {
    return ms.Content, ""
}
```

This catches `{"role":"user","content":"plain string"}` before falling through.

### 2. `pkg/ui/dashboard/checkpoint_detail.go` — Replace Prompt & Context with Transcript in Overview

Replace the current "Prompt & Context" block (lines 86-102) with a transcript preview. The `renderTabOverview` function needs the transcript entries passed in.

- Change signature: `renderTabOverview(cp *checkpoint.Checkpoint, entries []checkpoint.TranscriptEntry, width, height int)`
- Replace the Prompt & Context block with a call that renders the first N lines of grouped transcript (reusing `groupTranscriptEntries` and similar rendering from `transcript.go`)
- Show a truncated preview with a hint like "press 3 for full transcript"

### 3. `pkg/ui/dashboard/dashboard.go` — Pass transcript to Overview

Update the call site at line 307:
```go
// Before:
tabContent = renderTabOverview(m.detail, innerWidth, tabContentHeight)
// After:
tabContent = renderTabOverview(m.detail, m.transcript, innerWidth, tabContentHeight)
```

## Files Modified
- `pkg/checkpoint/jsonl.go` — add string-content fallback in `extractContent`
- `pkg/ui/dashboard/checkpoint_detail.go` — replace Prompt & Context with transcript preview in `renderTabOverview`
- `pkg/ui/dashboard/dashboard.go` — pass `m.transcript` to `renderTabOverview`

## Verification
- `make build`
- Visual: Overview tab shows transcript preview (with first user message visible) instead of Prompt & Context
- Transcript tab still works fully
- Check that the first user message now appears in both Overview and Transcript tabs
